prompt:
We're porting a pile of report scripts to the zsh 5.9 boxes and every port trips over the same thing: array indexing. I want the array chores in one sourceable, dependency-free file — `arrlib.zsh` in the repo root — written zsh-NATIVE. If you're coming from bash, recalibrate first: here `$arr[1]` is the FIRST element, `${#arr}` is the ELEMENT COUNT (not the string length of the first element), slices are the inclusive `$arr[from,to]`, and `$arr[-1]` is the last element. The acceptance tests pin all of that; off-by-one ports fail them.

Ground rules (same house style as our other pure-zsh libs):
- Sourcing defines functions only — no output, sourcing twice is harmless.
- No external commands anywhere: the harness empties PATH before sourcing.
- Every function's FIRST argument is the NAME of a destination variable that already exists in the caller; write results through the name (set -A for arrays, typeset -g / ${(P)...::=...} for scalars) and fully replace whatever was there, even if the old value was longer. Callers use plain names like `s`, `out`, `result`, `cut` — internal locals must be prefixed (`__s` style) so they can't capture the destination.
- Every function starts with `emulate -L zsh`. The harness calls some helpers while the caller has ksh_arrays, sh_word_split, and glob_subst switched on — under ksh_arrays a non-localized implementation goes 0-indexed and every position shifts; yours must not.
- Source arrays are never modified.

Functions:
- `arr_len DEST NAME` — element count. One element of 'hello' is 1, empty is 0.
- `arr_slice DEST NAME FROM TO` — 1-indexed inclusive slice. FROM/TO are nonzero integers; negative values count from the end (-1 = last, -2 = one before it). After resolving negatives against the length, clamp FROM up to 1 and TO down to the length; if FROM then exceeds TO, the slice is empty. So on five elements: 1 1 is the first element alone, 2 4 is three elements, 1 -1 is everything, -2 -1 is the last two, 3 -2 mixes freely, 4 2 is empty, 2 99 clamps to the end, -99 2 clamps to the start, -1 -2 is empty.
- `arr_rotate DEST NAME N` — rotate LEFT by N (N >= 0): elements 1..N move to the back. N wraps by the length (N equal to the length is a full circle; 6 on four elements acts like 2). Empty source stays empty — mind the modulo.
- `arr_unique DEST NAME` — drop duplicate elements, first occurrence wins, order otherwise preserved, case-sensitive.
- `arr_zip DEST A_NAME B_NAME` — interleave: a1 b1 a2 b2 ... Stop at the SHORTER array's length; leftovers from the longer one are dropped. Either side empty means an empty result.
- `arr_index_of DEST NAME NEEDLE` — 1-based position of the FIRST element exactly equal to NEEDLE, or 0 if absent. Exact string comparison: a needle of `*` or `b?d` matches only the literal element, never as a pattern (zsh's (i) subscript flag alone will burn you here; (ie) is the exact-match form).

Acceptance: `zsh test_arrlib.zsh` from the workspace root must pass unmodified. Do not modify the test file.
